Cloud DevOps Engineer
About Me

The engineer behind
the deployment.

"Behind every smooth deployment is a DevOps Engineer who stayed when staging broke — I'm that engineer."

I'm a Cloud DevOps Engineer with 2+ years of hands-on experience designing and securing cloud infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, and containerized environments on AWS and Kubernetes. Based in Lagos, Nigeria.

What makes me different: I started my career with real physical infrastructure — configuring Cisco switches, commissioning power transformers, deploying access points. Most cloud engineers have never touched hardware. I understand the full stack, from data center floor to Kubernetes pod.

Recently certified as an ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Lead Auditor, I'm actively bridging DevOps and DevSecOps/GRC — building systems that are not just automated and observable, but secure and compliant by design.

AWS Solutions Architect – Associate KCNA Certified ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Lead Auditor AWS Cloud Practitioner OCI DevOps Professional Scrum Fundamentals

IT Risk

IT Risk & Security Monitoring

IT Operations · Device Lifecycle & Endpoint Management

How I Think

My engineering principles.

01

"If it's not observable, it's broken."

Every system I build is instrumented from day one. I don't wait for users to tell me something is wrong.

02

"Manual fixes are technical debt."

If I fixed it once by hand, I'm writing automation for it. Toil is a bug. Operational friction is a code smell.

03

"Every failure should be reproducible."

Post-mortems aren't blame sessions — they're data collection. I build chaos-ready systems because I've seen what happens when you don't.

04

"Security is infrastructure, not an afterthought."

My ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Lead Auditor cert isn't a pivot — it's the formalisation of how I've always thought. Compliance and reliability are the same goal.
